revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24644718927|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:気吹戸主神]]
{{Infobox deity}}


'''{{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|ja|気吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi no Kami|qq=}}''' (気吹戸主神) is a deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. This deity is also mentioned in the {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba|en|Ōharae no Kotoba|qid=Q11438317|lt=Ōharae Kotoba|1=Ōharae no Kotoba}}, where they are described as a god who creates strong winds over the seas to blow away sins and impurities<ref name="T">{{Cite book|和書|author=E. Mogi & M. Sonoda|title=Encyclopedia of Japanese Gods|publisher=Gakken|page=47}}</ref>. The name is also written as Ibukidonushi no Kami.

== Overview ==
Ibukidonushi no Kami is one of the {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Kami|qq=}}. Many traditions equate this deity with {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}}. According to the {{ill|Ōharae no Kotoba Goshaku|ja|大祓詞後釈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae no Kotoba Goshaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579168|1=Ōharae no Kotoba Goshaku}}, it is said: 
*"This god, being the spirit that rectifies and purifies all evils, encompasses everything from the beginning to the end of driving away and losing all impurities, starting from the rapids of the swift river down to the root country."*

== Analysis ==
* In the {{ill|Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|qq=}}, Ibukidonushi is identified as another name for Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami.
* The {{ill|Nakatomi Harai Kunkai|ja|中臣祓訓解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi Harai Kunkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135491602}}, traditionally attributed to {{ill|Kōbō Daishi|ja|弘法大師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbō Daishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 空海|1=Kūkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q222227}}, states: 
''' "This deity is named Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami, a god who hears and sees to correct matters."
* {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|qid=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}}, a scholar of {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|qid=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}}, offered a unique interpretation, equating {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} and {{ill|Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi-no-kami|lt=Magatsuhi no Kami|ja|禍津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatsuhi no Kami|qq=}} as the same deity. Following this logic, he interpreted Ibukidonushi no Kami as identical to Kamunaobi no Kami and Ōnaobi no Kami.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}


== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|直毘神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kamuna'obi|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunaobi no Kami|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ōnaobi no Kami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaobi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神}}
* {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}}
* {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|祓戸大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=}}
* {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|qid=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities|1=List of Japanese deities}}
